rows.requirement: Scalar control flow and calls execute in a direct-native binary without generated Rust.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Preserve native scalar lowering and generated_rust-null artifact evidence.

rows.requirement: Local packages, path dependencies, workspaces, and lockfile validation are executable.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Package graph inspection must retain deterministic local identities and lock state.

rows.requirement: Compilation never executes runtime effects or freezes runtime inputs into replay binaries.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Build JSON must expose lowering mode, runtime support, and fail-closed diagnostics.

rows.requirement: A backend-neutral executable MIR represents control, values, effects, ownership, and provenance and drives native lowering.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Agents need deterministic MIR blocks, effects, ownership operations, spans, and semantic provenance.

rows.requirement: Allocation, move, clone, borrow, drop, recursive cleanup, and resource handles have target-neutral runtime semantics.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Inspection must expose allocation effects, ownership transfer, drops, and resource obligations.

rows.requirement: Runtime-sized sequences allocate, grow, index, mutate, iterate, and clean up from runtime-origin data.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose allocation, capacity, element ownership, bounds, origin, and cleanup.

rows.requirement: Runtime-origin strings and slices cross calls, returns, aggregates, and mutable aliases correctly.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose storage, origin, length/capacity, ownership, aliasing, call/return ABI, and cleanup.

rows.requirement: Runtime maps and sets provide growable storage, explicit equality/hashing, deterministic iteration, mutation, collision bounds, and cleanup.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose key/value types, equality/hash/order contracts, collisions, allocation, lifecycle, and limits.

rows.requirement: Dynamic non-Copy aggregates cross calls, returns, storage, mutation, and cleanup without static projection shortcuts.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose value layouts, lifecycle, argument/return lowering, and unsupported shapes.

rows.requirement: One MIR pass proves moves, borrows, reborrows, joins, drops, and resource cleanup.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Provide deterministic moves, loans, origins, joins, drops, and rejected paths.

rows.requirement: Runtime text supports explicit UTF-8 validation, slicing, split, lines, scalar iteration, and conversion.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Distinguish byte/scalar operations, validity, runtime origin, and allocation.

rows.requirement: A stable iteration protocol, for loops, break, and continue work over runtime collections and text.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose iteration collection, mode, element ownership, and loop evidence.

rows.requirement: Typed paths, metadata, deterministic traversal, binary I/O, atomic update, and secure temporary resources are capability-scoped.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose scoped roots, path origin, operation, handle lifetime, and denial.

rows.requirement: The running program receives argv, scoped environment, stdin, cwd, distinct output streams, exit, and cleanup at runtime rather than build time.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Distinguish build and runtime inputs; expose streams, capture, exit reason, artifact, and cleanup.

rows.requirement: Child processes use argv-safe execution with pipes, signals, cancellation, terminal state, and precise authority.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose executable authority, argv origins, stdio, signal, cancellation, and terminal dependencies.

rows.requirement: Runtime tasks, cancellation, fair select, bounded channels, backpressure, and accepted synchronization semantics are production-capable.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose task hierarchy, cancellation, channel capacity/backpressure, sendability, and resources.

rows.requirement: Readiness-based sockets, timers, signals, cancellation, and backpressure use portable runtime adapters.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose awaited resource, readiness, deadline, cancellation owner, buffers, and adapter.

rows.requirement: Connect, listen, DNS, accepted-peer, and dynamic endpoint authority are separately modeled and enforced.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose network direction, dynamic origin, resolution, rule, and decision.

rows.requirement: HTTP requests support methods, headers, byte bodies, TLS policy, redirects, limits, and cancellation.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose method, authority, limits, TLS policy, effects, and response evidence.

rows.requirement: A long-running HTTP server supports dynamic requests, external authorized bind, backpressure, limits, and graceful drain.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose bind authority, handler node, limits, in-flight work, shutdown, and responses.

rows.requirement: Runtime dynamic JSON and typed codecs preserve nested values, limits, deterministic output, and structured error paths.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose codec schema, field mappings, limits, runtime origin, and error paths.

rows.requirement: Logs, traces, metrics, propagation, redaction, bounded sinks, and shutdown flushing are operationally sound.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Link effects, trace context, metrics, redaction, sinks, and evidence.

rows.requirement: A capability-scoped SQLite package supports prepared statements, typed rows, transactions, recovery, and lifecycle safety.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose database authority, transaction scope, statement schema, effects, and evidence.

rows.requirement: Safe versioned providers use opaque handles and owned/borrowed buffers without raw-pointer escape.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose provider version, handles, capabilities, lifecycle, target support, and trust.

rows.requirement: Extended CI executes product evidence instead of rerunning only fast checks.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Emit exact-head target, duration, failure class, and artifact paths.

rows.requirement: Linux x86-64 and macOS arm64 have declared, executable native toolchain evidence.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Report supported state, missing features, ABI/linker/runtime requirements, and evidence per target.

rows.requirement: Users install reproducible signed compiler artifacts and run the toolchain without Rust or Cargo.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose release digest, target, provenance, contract versions, install, upgrade, and rollback state.

rows.requirement: Runtime-origin hashing, MAC, secure random bytes, AEAD, and signatures use vetted supported-target providers with explicit secret lifecycle and failure policy.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose operation, algorithm, provider/version, capability, redacted key identity, buffer provenance, target, and evidence.

rows.requirement: Language, stdlib, CLI, packages, ABI, schemas, and artifacts have versioning and migration policy.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Classify changed public nodes, severity, deprecation, and migration evidence.

rows.requirement: Consumers verify asymmetric publisher identity, archive integrity, provenance, rotation, and revocation.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose signer, digest, provenance, trust decision, and stable reason codes.

rows.requirement: Registry dependencies resolve deterministically into trusted cache, lockfile v2, offline builds, and vendored sources.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Explain requested and selected versions, trust, cache, and resolution decisions.

rows.requirement: Formatting is syntax-aware, idempotent, comment-preserving, error-tolerant, and semantics-preserving.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Provide precise edits and unchanged semantic graph digest.

rows.requirement: A persistent package-aware LSP provides diagnostics, navigation, symbols, signatures, and completion.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose workspace generation, indexed nodes, request versions, capabilities, latency, and diagnostics.

rows.requirement: Typed public API docs include stable symbols, links, search, source, capabilities, and executable doctests.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Link public symbols to source, semantic nodes, capabilities, and evidence.

rows.requirement: Fuzzing, real coverage, mutation, and complexity ratchets protect compiler and runtime contracts.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Map failures to semantic area, seed, reproducer, span, and governing issue.

rows.requirement: Tests execute real seeded property cases with shrinking and benchmarks execute workload entrypoints with enforced baselines.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose stable case IDs, seeds, minimal failures, timings, flake state, and baseline delta.

rows.requirement: Build profiles, optimization, module-level caching, parallelism, and reproducibility are explicit and scalable.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Explain every cache hit, miss, invalidation, queue, profile, and critical path.

rows.requirement: Native AxiOM DWARF, real process-backed DAP, and profiling map runtime state to source and stable symbols.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Report binary digest, symbols, source generation, target, breakpoints, runtime state, and profiles.

rows.requirement: Released CLI, worker, and HTTP API workloads pass runtime sensitivity, load, recovery, security, and operational gates.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose workload, target, release digest, SLOs, faults, recovery, resources, evidence, and blockers.

rows.requirement: One built compiler-scale AxiOM package processes different runtime source inputs through real native execution.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Link runtime source, compiler packages, MIR, lowering, capabilities, outputs, and artifact.

rows.requirement: Diagnostics, syntax, package graph, HIR, MIR, stdlib, backend contracts/native runtime, commands, evidence, doc, and LSP compiler packages are AxiOM-owned.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Report package-by-package ownership, coexistence, parity, rollback, and Rust-capture evidence.

rows.requirement: Compiler diagnostics, catalogs, spans, recovery hints, and protocol adapters are implemented by AxiOM-owned packages.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose AxiOM-owned codes, spans, recovery, source identity, and Rust-disable parity.

rows.requirement: Lexer, parser, comments, macros, recovery, and concrete source spans execute from AxiOM-owned compiler.syntax.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose tokens, parse/recovery nodes, macros, spans, parity, and Rust-disable state.

rows.requirement: Manifest, lockfile, workspace, dependency, registry, and source loading execute from AxiOM-owned package graph code.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose source/package identities, resolution, trust, diagnostics, parity, and Rust-disable state.

rows.requirement: Name/type resolution, capabilities, properties, ownership, and borrow analysis execute from AxiOM-owned compiler.hir.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose typed nodes, capabilities, moves/loans, properties, parity, and Rust-disable state.

rows.requirement: Executable MIR construction, verification, intrinsic planning, and backend inputs execute from AxiOM-owned compiler.mir.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose AxiOM-owned MIR, verification, backend plans, parity, and Rust-disable state.

rows.requirement: The compiler stdlib catalog, modules, capability/effect declarations, and intrinsic/provider bindings are AxiOM-owned.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose module/symbol catalog, capabilities, effects, bindings, digests, parity, and Rust-disable state.

rows.requirement: Backend target/effect/artifact contracts are AxiOM-owned and the legacy generated-Rust package is retired under a human cutover.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose target contracts, unsupported semantics, legacy inventory/removal, rollback, and cutover evidence.

rows.requirement: Native backend/runtime orchestration executes from AxiOM-owned packages consuming MIR, target, provider, lifecycle, and artifact contracts.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose native provider/target selection, runtime lowering, artifacts, parity, and Rust-disable state.

rows.requirement: Compiler commands, evidence, documentation, LSP, and DAP services execute from AxiOM-owned packages.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose command/service ownership, exact runtime path, protocol/evidence parity, and Rust-disable state.

rows.requirement: A trusted genesis snapshot builds and tests the next compiler offline with no Cargo or rustc after genesis and reaches fixpoint.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose snapshot trust, predecessor, target, compiler source set, process evidence, and fixpoint.

rows.requirement: The exact reviewed candidate satisfies language, source ownership, snapshot, release, provenance, rollback, and live governance gates.
rows.agentInspectionImpact: Expose exact candidate head, all evidence tiers, approvals, blockers, and human decision.
